title: Living in Eureka, MO — Complete 2026 Guide
content:
Eureka is a growing West County suburb along I-44, 30 miles from downtown STL. Spans 10.7 sq miles with ~14,000 residents.

Real Estate: Median sale price $402,500. Median 32 days on market. Sale-to-list ratio 99.7%. Range: $75K-$1.8M. Median household income: $110K+.

Neighborhoods:
- Old Towne Historic District: Late 1800s-early 1900s homes on Central Ave. Highest flood exposure. Joe Boccardi's pizza, antique shops.
- Northeastern Eureka: 1970s-2000s subdivisions (Elk Trails, Emerald Forest, Deerpath). Community pools.
- The Legends: Upscale, anchored by Legends Country Club. $400K-$900K+. HOA $1K-$2K/yr. The Reserve offers new custom construction.
- Crescent & Pevely Farms: High-end golf communities, custom homes typically $1M+. Private enclave feel.
- Deer Run Estates: Blufftop custom homes on 1+ acres with views and privacy.
- Southern Eureka (Jefferson County): Newer developments, lower property taxes. Windswept Farms (550 homes, fiber internet, USDA loan eligible). Mirasol (community pool). Polo Grounds (new construction, lake views).

Schools: Rockwood R-IV District (#4 in MO, all 29 schools A- or higher). Eureka Senior High (#26 in MO, ~1,651 students, AP + agriculture + performing arts). Elementary: Blevins, Eureka, Geggie, Ridge Meadows. Middle: LaSalle Springs (16:1 ratio). Private: St. Mark's Lutheran, Sacred Heart, Heritage Classical Christian. NOTE: Some Jefferson County addresses may fall under Northwest R-I district instead of Rockwood.

Commute: Downtown STL 32 min, Clayton 28 min, Chesterfield 25 min, Kirkwood 20 min, Wildwood 12 min. Average 25-30 min, 45+ peak hours.

Outdoor Recreation: 5.5-mile Flat Creek Trail loop. Route 66 State Park (424 acres, 10+ miles trails). Five golf courses (~90+ holes). Golf cart culture prominent. Route 66 runs through town.

Things to Do: Old Towne (Joe Boccardi's, O'Dell's Irish Pub, La Finca Coffeehouse, antique shops). Eureka Days festival, Concert on Central summer series. Berry Park (premier playground). Timbers of Eureka (30K sqft rec facility — gym, pool, cycling, mountain bike park). Brookdale Farms (300 acres — petting zoo, hayrides, corn maze, Balloon Glowdeo). Six Flags (being rebranded under Enchanted Parks by end 2026).

Development: New Aldi (Feb 2026). Michael A. Wiegand Justice Center ($11.5M, opened 2025). Rockwood Meadows (56-unit rental townhomes, approved March 2026).

Best for: Families prioritizing Rockwood schools, outdoor enthusiasts, remote workers. Trade-off: more house/land/schools for less walkability and nightlife.
